en.title: AI Documentation Workflow
en.content:
AI Documentation Workflow

Roles and collaboration model

Codex (primary documentation builder)

- Scans repository and verifies technical claims against source files.
- Builds and maintains docs architecture in .
- Produces comprehensive, structured technical pages.

Copilot (translation and small sections)

- Translates English source into Spanish and Basque.
- Handles small targeted expansions and formatting refinements.
- Should not modify core technical claims without verification.

Claude (review and editorial QA)

- Reviews correctness, coherence, and readability.
- Flags gaps, contradictions, and missing references.
- Recommends structural improvements for maintainability.

Non-negotiable rules

- No AI agent should invent system behavior.
- All uncertain areas must be explicitly marked as Needs verification.
- Every technical statement should be traceable to current repository code.

Suggested handoff sequence

1. Codex writes/updates English source pages.
2. Claude reviews and requests corrections.
3. Copilot translates dictionaries/content once source is stable.
4. Codex performs final consistency pass and publishes.

Translation next steps (for Copilot)

- Translate all strings in .
- Translate all strings in .
- Optionally split content files by locale once internationalized routing is introduced.

en.title: Docs Audit
en.content:
Docs Audit

This page tracks the current state of the documentation site: what was verified, what is still unconfirmed, known risks, and architecture decisions for future maintainers.

What was verified

The following claims were cross-referenced against the actual codebase under , , and :

- Monorepo structure: three packages (, , ) under pnpm workspaces. Verified from .
- Backend endpoint inventory: all endpoints in the API reference were verified against  files.
- Auth flows: access token (Bearer JWT, 15 min) + refresh token (HTTP-only cookie, 7 days) confirmed from  and .
- Package state machine:  confirmed from .
- Email side effects on status change: confirmed from .
- Token types: , , ,  confirmed from .
- Database name inconsistency:  creates database as ;  documents it as . Marked as Needs verification in environment docs.
- Google Directions origin: coordinates hardcoded in  — confirmed risk documented in security and environment pages.
- Frontend API client file paths: all  paths are referenced as documented; exact TypeScript type locations are verified from .

What is still unverified (Needs verification)

The following items could not be confirmed without access to a running environment or full integration test:

- Exact request/response payload schemas for admin-only endpoints that the current frontend does not call (, , , etc.).
- Pagination defaults for list endpoints (, , ).
- Error response variants beyond  — full error shape per status code.
- Production domain and CORS configuration (documented as risk, not confirmed).
- Activation URL: docs note it reuses ; exact behavior in a live environment is unverified.
- Frontend admin-facing UI screens: some screen locations referenced in domain/frontend pages could not be pinned to specific file paths.

Known risks

| Severity | Risk | Reference |
| --- | --- | --- |
| High |  has a hardcoded fallback in  — if not set in production, the fallback is publicly visible in the repo | Security page, CLAUDE.md §4.2 |
| High |  has the same hardcoded fallback problem | Security page, CLAUDE.md §4.3 |
| Medium |  forwards internal error messages to HTTP responses in 500 cases | CLAUDE.md §4.5 |
| Medium | Rate limiting packages installed () but not implemented | CLAUDE.md §7.2 |
| Low | MySQL env vars are not validated at startup — silent failures possible | CLAUDE.md §4.4 |
| Low |  uses TypeScript  assertion without a startup guard | CLAUDE.md §4.10 |

Docs site architecture decisions

- Custom layout — the docs do not use  layout. Instead, a fully custom sidebar, header, and content layout is implemented in . This was chosen to allow full design control.
- Locale routing — locale is the first URL segment (, , ). Middleware at  redirects locale-less paths to .
- Search — implemented as a static client-side page-title search via . No full-text index. Ctrl+K / Cmd+K opens the search modal.
- Table of contents —  reads DOM headings after mount (scoped to ). Headings get stable IDs from the  function in .
- Mobile TOC —  renders a button below the header that opens a bottom drawer with the TOC and a shortcut to search.
- i18n — dictionaries live in . Adding a new nav item requires updating  interface and all three locale files.
- MDX heading IDs — , ,  MDX components are overridden in  to auto-generate  attributes via .

Build and search status

| Feature | Status |
| --- | --- |
| Root  redirect to  | Working via  + middleware |
| Locale-prefixed routing | Working — , ,  |
| Locale fallback (e.g.  → ) | Working via  |
| Per-page TOC (desktop) | Working — right-side sticky via  |
| Per-page TOC (mobile) | Working — bottom drawer via  |
| Ctrl+K search | Working — static title search via  |
| Heading anchor links | Working — IDs generated via  in  |
| Code block copy button | Working — via  |
| Mobile sidebar | Working — via  |
| View transitions | Working — CSS view transitions on page navigation |

How future agents should update docs

1. Adding a new backend endpoint: update . Add request/response examples. Verify from the actual  and  files. Mark anything unverified with .
2. Adding a new frontend page or hook: update .
3. Adding a new doc section: create  and . Add the section to . Add a nav item in  and update all three i18n dictionaries.
4. Creating ES/EUS counterparts: create the content files under  and . If full translation is not complete, add a translated header and mark the page as pending.
5. Fixing a broken link: all internal links should be locale-prefixed (e.g. ). Locale-less links also work via middleware redirect, but explicit locale links are preferred.
6. Keeping docs truthful: never document behavior that cannot be verified from the codebase. Use  callouts with "Needs verification" for anything uncertain.

en.title: Deployment
en.content:
Deployment

Backend deployment notes

- Requires MySQL connectivity and all critical env vars.
- Must set production  and  overrides.
- Requires functional Resend API key for auth + package emails.
- Requires Google API key for route creation/geocoding.

Frontend deployment notes

- In production mode, API base URL falls back to .
- HERE maps key must be configured in .
- Frontend requests rely on cookies () for refresh flow.

API domain assumptions

Frontend assumes backend is reachable with CORS/cookie-compatible settings from deployed frontend domain.

Cookie/SameSite/Secure implications

- Backend refresh cookie:  in production.
- Frontend access cookie: ,  in production.
- If domains/subdomains are misconfigured, refresh flow may fail silently.

Known deployment pitfalls

- CORS policy not allowing credentialed requests.
- Inconsistent TLS setup causing Secure cookies not to persist.
- API URL mismatch between frontend environment and backend host.
- Missing Google/Resend secrets causing runtime failures in route creation/email dispatch.

en.title: Project Overview
en.content:
Project Overview

What PakAG is

PakAG is a delivery operations platform focused on package orchestration and delivery visibility. The system includes:

- an internal operations app (frontend) for admins/distributors,
- an API backend (business logic + data persistence), and
- a public package-tracking endpoint.

Problem it solves

PakAG centralizes package lifecycle management:

- package creation and assignment,
- route planning and stop progression,
- delivery-state transitions and status history,
- notification emails,
- recipient-facing tracking.

Backend/frontend relationship

-  consumes backend endpoints through Axios clients under .
-  exposes Next.js Route Handlers under  and enforces auth/roles with JWT bearer tokens.
- Frontend keeps access tokens in a cookie; backend manages refresh token rotation via HTTP-only cookie.

Product actors

- Admin: user/account management, package and route administration.
- Distributor: daily route execution, status changes, delivery operations.
- Recipient: package-tracking view (token-based, no account login).

> [!WARNING]
> Some UI labels are currently Basque in the frontend implementation. This docs site is intentionally in English for onboarding consistency.

en.title: Troubleshooting
en.content:
Troubleshooting

Backend won't start

Symptom:  or similar on .

Cause: Path aliases not resolved or  paths not matching.

Fix: Verify  at  has  under . If missing, check that  extends the correct base.

---

Missing or undefined environment variables

Symptom:  values in logs, DB connect errors, email send errors, JWT errors like .

Fix:
1. Copy  to  in .
2. Fill in all values — do not leave anything as-is from the example.
3. Restart the dev server after changes.

Critical vars that have hardcoded fallbacks (unsafe in prod, but will silently work in dev):
-  — uses known fallback if unset
-  — uses known fallback if unset

Vars with no fallback that will crash at runtime:
-  — email send will fail
-  — geocoding and route optimization will fail
- MySQL vars — DB pool will fail at first query

---

Database connection errors

Symptom: , , or  at runtime.

Checklist:
1. MySQL server is running on .
2.  /  credentials are correct.
3. The database exists.  creates it as  — run  if needed.
4.  env var matches the actual database name ( vs  — they differ between  and ).
5. SSL setting:  sets . If your MySQL instance requires a specific cert, adjust this.

---

TypeScript check fails ( errors)

Symptom: Type errors when running  in .

Common causes and fixes:

| Error | Fix |
| --- | --- |
|  | Check that the DTO interface and the types file are in sync |
|  | No  allowed — add an explicit interface |
|  | Check  paths |
| Type error in  | Verify  return type is used correctly |

---

401 on every request after login

Symptom: Login succeeds (returns ), but subsequent API calls return 401.

Checklist:
1. The frontend is storing the access token correctly in the  cookie.
2. The Axios request interceptor is reading from the cookie and setting .
3. The token isn't expired — access token lifetime is 15 minutes.
4. The backend is on a different origin — check CORS config and .

---

Refresh token not working / user repeatedly kicked to login

Symptom: User is logged out after 15 minutes despite  being active.

Checklist:
1. Open DevTools → Application → Cookies. Confirm  cookie exists with  flag.
2. Confirm  is not blocked by CORS. In dev, backend must allow the frontend origin with .
3. Confirm the refresh token row is not revoked/expired in the  table.
4. Check clock skew — if server time and client time differ by more than the token lifetime, tokens may appear expired immediately.
5. Confirm  is set in the Axios client.

---

CORS errors

Symptom:  header missing, preflight fails.

Fix: In  (or equivalent CORS middleware), ensure:
- The frontend origin is allowed explicitly (not  when using credentials).
-  is set.
- Preflight  requests return 200.

In production, both apps must be on HTTPS for  cookies to work.

---

Email not sent after package creation / status change

Symptom: No email arrives, but the operation succeeds.

Checklist:
1.  is set and valid.
2. The sender domain () is verified in Resend.
3. Check Resend dashboard for send failures or bounces.
4. Status change emails are conditional — only specific transitions trigger emails. See Domain Model for the email trigger table.

---

Route creation fails (Google Maps error)

Symptom:  returns 500 or an error related to Directions API.

Checklist:
1.  is set and the key has Directions API and Geocoding API enabled in Google Cloud Console.
2. Billing is active on the Google Cloud project.
3. The API key has no IP/referer restriction that blocks the backend server IP.
4. Package addresses were geocoded successfully on creation — if / are 0 or NULL, geocoding failed at package creation time.

---

Geocoding returns wrong location

Symptom: Package appears at wrong coordinates on the map.

Cause: Google Geocoding matched a different address, or the address is ambiguous.

Fix: Check the package's  row in the DB. Correct / manually if needed via a direct DB update (no endpoint currently exposes raw coordinate editing).

---

Route optimization exceeds max stops

Symptom: Route creation is rejected even with fewer packages than expected.

Cause: The route service adds carryover packages (assigned packages from previous days for the same distributor) to the stop list automatically. Total stops are capped at 20.

Fix: Reduce  in the request to leave room for carryover, or clear the distributor's backlog first.

---

Package status log missing after update

Symptom:  returns no logs after  call.

Cause: Log insertion happens in . If it throws and is caught silently, the status update may succeed but no log is written.

Fix: Check backend console for errors from . Verify DB constraints allow the log insert (e.g.,  user exists).

---

Frontend build fails

Symptom:  errors with TypeScript or module errors.

Fix:

---

Docs site search not finding a page

Symptom: Ctrl+K search returns no results for a known page.

Cause: Search is static and based on  titles in . If you add a new page without adding it to , it won't appear in search.

Fix: Add the new page to  in  and update all three i18n dictionaries. See Docs Audit for the full procedure.

---

pnpm version conflicts

Symptom:  or workspace script not found.

Fix:

All workspace package commands must be run from the monorepo root using  flags or the root-level scripts in .

es.title: Flujo de trabajo de documentación con IA
es.content:
Flujo de trabajo de documentación con IA

Roles y modelo de colaboración

Codex (constructor principal de documentación)

- Escanea el repositorio y verifica afirmaciones técnicas contra los archivos fuente.
- Construye y mantiene la arquitectura de documentación en .
- Produce páginas técnicas completas y estructuradas.

Copilot (traducción y secciones pequeñas)

- Traduce el inglés fuente al español y al euskara.
- Gestiona expansiones pequeñas y refinamientos de formato.
- No debe modificar afirmaciones técnicas básicas sin verificación.

Claude (revisión y control de calidad editorial)

- Revisa la corrección, coherencia y legibilidad.
- Señala lagunas, contradicciones y referencias faltantes.
- Recomienda mejoras estructurales para la mantenibilidad.

Reglas no negociables

- Ningún agente IA debe inventar comportamiento del sistema.
- Todas las áreas inciertas deben marcarse explícitamente como Requiere verificación.
- Cada afirmación técnica debe ser rastreable al código actual del repositorio.

Secuencia de traspaso sugerida

1. Codex escribe/actualiza las páginas fuente en inglés.
2. Claude revisa y solicita correcciones.
3. Copilot traduce los diccionarios/contenido una vez que la fuente es estable.
4. Codex realiza el pase final de consistencia y publica.

Despliegue

Notas de despliegue del backend

- Requiere conectividad MySQL y todas las variables de entorno críticas.
- Se deben definir las sobreescrituras de producción de  y .
- Requiere una clave API de Resend funcional para emails de autenticación y paquetes.
- Requiere clave de Google API para la creación de rutas y geocodificación.

Notas de despliegue del frontend

- En modo producción, la URL base de la API cae en .
- La clave HERE Maps debe estar configurada en .
- Las solicitudes del frontend dependen de cookies () para el flujo de refresco.

Supuestos de dominio API

El frontend asume que el backend es accesible con configuración CORS/cookie compatible desde el dominio del frontend desplegado.

Implicaciones de Cookie/SameSite/Secure

- Cookie de refresco del backend:  en producción.
- Cookie de acceso del frontend: ,  en producción.
- Si los dominios/subdominios están mal configurados, el flujo de refresco puede fallar silenciosamente.

Problemas conocidos de despliegue

- La política CORS no permite solicitudes con credenciales.
- Configuración TLS inconsistente que impide que las cookies Secure persistan.
- Discrepancia de URL de API entre el entorno del frontend y el host del backend.
- Falta de secretos de Google/Resend que causa fallos en tiempo de ejecución en la creación de rutas/envío de emails.

es.title: Visión general del proyecto
es.content:
Visión general del proyecto

Qué es PakAG

PakAG es una plataforma de operaciones de entrega centrada en la orquestación de paquetes y la visibilidad de las entregas. El sistema incluye:

- una aplicación de operaciones interna (frontend) para administradores y distribuidores,
- un backend API (lógica de negocio + persistencia de datos), y
- un endpoint público de seguimiento de paquetes.

Problema que resuelve

PakAG centraliza la gestión del ciclo de vida de los paquetes:

- creación y asignación de paquetes,
- planificación de rutas y progresión de paradas,
- transiciones de estado de entrega e historial de estados,
- correos electrónicos de notificación,
- seguimiento para el destinatario.

Relación backend/frontend

-  consume los endpoints del backend mediante clientes Axios en .
-  expone Next.js Route Handlers en  y aplica autenticación/roles con tokens JWT Bearer.
- El frontend guarda los tokens de acceso en una cookie; el backend gestiona la rotación del token de refresco mediante cookie HTTP-only.

Actores del producto

- Admin: gestión de usuarios/cuentas, administración de paquetes y rutas.
- Distribuidor: ejecución de la ruta diaria, cambios de estado, operaciones de entrega.
- Destinatario: vista de seguimiento de paquetes (basada en token, sin inicio de sesión).

> [!WARNING]
> Algunos textos de la interfaz están actualmente en euskara en la implementación del frontend. Este sitio de documentación está intencionalmente en el idioma seleccionado para facilitar la incorporación de nuevos desarrolladores.

Flujo del token de acceso

- El backend emite un token JWT de acceso al hacer login/refresh.

-  valida la expiración del JWT en el navegador antes de permitir la navegación protegida.

Flujo de la cookie de token de refresco

- El backend establece  como cookie HTTP-only mediante .
- Al refrescar, el backend revoca la fila del token antiguo e inserta un nuevo registro de token de refresco.
- El logout revoca el token de refresco actual y limpia la cookie.

- Cookie de token de acceso: , ,  en producción.

es.title: Solución de problemas
es.content:
Solución de problemas

Variables de entorno faltantes

Síntomas: errores al arrancar, fallos de clave indefinida, fallos de conexión a DB.

- Verificar las variables del backend desde .
- Verificar  del frontend y la clave HERE.

Discrepancia de URL de API

Síntomas: errores 404/Red del frontend al host incorrecto.

- Confirmar el  en tiempo de ejecución en .

Problemas de CORS/cookie/sesión

Síntomas: 401 repetidos, el refresco no restaura la sesión.

- Asegurarse de que las solicitudes se envíen con credenciales.
- Asegurarse de que el backend permita credenciales cross-origin.
- Asegurarse de HTTPS en producción para soporte de cookie Secure.

Bucles de expiración JWT

Síntomas: el usuario es expulsado a  con frecuencia.

- Comprobar la duración máxima del token de acceso (15m) y el intervalo de refresco (12m).
- Inspeccionar el almacenamiento de cookies del navegador y el desfase de reloj.

Token de refresco no funciona

- Confirmar que la cookie  existe y se envía.
- Confirmar que  no está bloqueado por CORS/proxy.
- Confirmar que la fila del token no está expirada/revocada en la DB.

Errores de conexión MySQL

- Comprobar env de host/puerto/usuario/contraseña/base de datos.
- Validar la ruta de red desde el tiempo de ejecución a la DB.
- Validar los requisitos SSL frente a la configuración actual .

Errores de Resend/email

- Confirmar la validez de  y la configuración del dominio del remitente ().

Errores de Google Directions/Geocoding

- Confirmar que  está activa para las APIs requeridas.
- Comprobar los límites de cuota y el estado de facturación.

Errores de build de Next.js

- Ejecutar los comandos de build del paquete local para aislar los problemas de backend/frontend/docs.

Problemas con el gestor de paquetes

- Usar  de forma consistente en este monorepo.
- Reinstalar con  en la raíz si el lockfile/workspace diverge.

eus.content:
AI dokumentazio fluxua

Rolak eta lankidetza-eredua

Codex (dokumentazioaren eraikitzaile nagusia)

- Biltegia eskaneatu eta iturri-fitxategien aurka egiaztapen teknikoak egiten ditu.
- -ko dokumentazio-arkitektura eraiki eta mantentzen du.
- Orrialde tekniko oso eta egituratutakoak sortzen ditu.

Copilot (itzulpena eta atal txikiak)

- Iturri ingelesa espainolera eta euskarara itzultzen du.
- Zabalpen txikiak eta formatu-fintzeak kudeatzen ditu.
- Ez luke egiaztapenik gabe oinarrizko aldarrikapen teknikoak aldatu behar.

Claude (berrikuspen eta argitalpen-kalitate kontrola)

- Zuzentasuna, koherentzia eta irakurgarritasuna berrikusi.
- Hutsuneak, kontraesanak eta erreferentzia falta seinalatzen ditu.
- Mantengarritasun-hobetze estrukturalak gomendatzen ditu.

Negoziaezineko arauak

- Inongo AI agenteak ez du sistema-portaera asmatu behar.
- Ziurgabeak diren arlo guztiak Egiaztapena behar du gisa modu esplizituan markatu behar dira.
- Aldarrikapen tekniko bakoitza uneko biltegiaren kodean trazagarria izan behar da.

Iradokitako eskualdatze-sekuentzia

1. Codex-ek ingelese iturri-orriak idatzi/eguneratzen ditu.
2. Claude-k berrikusi eta zuzenketak eskatzen ditu.
3. Copilot-ek hiztegiak/edukia itzultzen ditu iturria egonkor denean.
4. Codex-ek azken koherentzia-pasa egiten du eta argitaratzen du.

eus.title: Proiektuaren ikuspegi orokorra
eus.content:
Proiektuaren ikuspegi orokorra

PakAG zer den

PakAG pakete-orkestrazioan eta entrega-ikusgarritasunean zentratutako entrega-eragiketarako plataforma bat da. Sistemak honako hauek ditu:

- barne-eragiketen aplikazio bat (frontend) administratzaile eta banatzaileentzat,
- API backend bat (negozio-logika + datuen iraunkortasuna), eta
- pakete-jarraipen endpoint publiko bat.

Konpontzen duen arazoa

PakAG paketeen bizitza-zikloaren kudeaketa zentralizatzen du:

- paketeak sortu eta esleitzea,
- ibilbideen planifikazioa eta geralekuen aurrerapena,
- entrega-egoeraren trantsizioak eta egoera-historia,
- jakinarazpen-emailak,
- hartzailearen pakete-jarraipena.

Backend/frontend erlazioa

- -ek backend-eko endpoint-ak kontsumitzen ditu -ko Axios bezeroetan.
- -k Next.js Route Handlers zabaltzen ditu -n eta autentifikazioa/rolak aplikatzen ditu JWT Bearer tokenekin.
- Frontend-ak sarbide-tokenak cookie batean gordetzen ditu; backend-ak freskatze-tokenaren errotazioa kudeatzen du HTTP-only cookie bidez.

Produktuaren aktore nagusiak

- Admin: erabiltzaile/kontu kudeaketa, pakete eta ibilbide administrazioa.
- Banatzailea: eguneroko ibilbidearen exekuzioa, egoera-aldaketak, entrega-eragiketak.
- Hartzailea: pakete-jarraipen ikuspegia (token-oinarritua, saioa hasteko konturik gabe).

> [!WARNING]
> UI etiketa batzuk euskaraz daude uneko frontend inplementazioan. Dokumentazio-gune hau aukeratutako hizkuntzan dago garatzaile berriak azkar txertatzeko.

eus.title: Arazoak konpontzea
eus.content:
Arazoak konpontzea

Ingurune-aldagaiak falta

Sintomak: abiatzeko erroreak, gako-definitu gabeko hutsegiteak, DB konexio-hutsegiteak.

- Backend aldagaiak egiaztatu -tik.
- Frontend  eta HERE gakoa egiaztatu.

API URL desegokitasuna

Sintomak: frontend 404/Sare-erroreak host okerrari.

- Exekuzio-denboran  berretsi -n.

CORS/cookie/saio arazoak

Sintomak: 401 errepikakorra, freskatze-ak saioa berreskuratzen ez.

- Eskaerak kredentzial-rekin bidaltzen direla ziurtatu.
- Backend-ak jatorri arteko kredentzialak onartzen dituela ziurtatu.
- Produkzioan HTTPS ziurtatu Secure cookie onarpenerako.

JWT iraungitzeko begiztak

Sintomak: erabiltzailea -era maiz bidal.

- Sarbide-token gehienezko adina (15m) eta freskatze-tartea (12m) egiaztatu.
- Nabigatzailearen cookie biltegiratzea eta erloju-desfasea aztertu.

Freskatze-tokena ez dabil

-  cookiea existitzen dela eta bidaltzen dela berretsi.
-  CORS/proxy-ak blokeatuta ez dagoela berretsi.
- Token-errenkada ez dagoela iraungituta/errebokaturik DB-an berretsi.

MySQL konexio-erroreak

- Host/portua/erabiltzailea/pasahitza/datu-basea env egiaztatu.
- Exekuzio-denboratik DB-ra sareko bidea balioztatui.
- SSL eskakizunak uneko  konfigurazioarekin alderatu.

Resend/email erroreak

-  balizkotasuna eta igorlearen domeinuaren konfigurazioa () berretsi.

Google Directions/Geocoding erroreak

-  beharrezko APIetarako aktibo dagoela berretsi.
- Kuota-mugak eta fakturatze-egoera egiaztatu.

Next.js build-erroreak

- Pakete-tokiko build komandoak exekutatu backend/frontend/docs arazoak isolatzeko.

Pakete-kudeatzailearen arazoak

-  erabili koherenteki monorepo honetan.
- Lockfile/workspace desbideratu bada -ekin berrinstalatu sustraian.
